mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 70% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production declines within tightening cycles, with a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation indicates real rate compression that boosts equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL MoM change <= 0.1
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation accelerates above 0.1% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth signals immediate economic contraction that forces rapid portfolio deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns during confirmed GDP contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable retail sales indicates resilient supply chain throughput that supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of occurrences during post-shock recovery periods, or loses predictive power when supply constraints dominate demand.
